017package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static java.util.Comparator.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ElementFlag.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
027
028import java.util.*;
029import java.util.function.*;
030
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.concurrent.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.logging.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
035
036/**
037 * Utility class for creating beans through constructors, creator methods, and builders.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * Uses a {@link BeanStore} to find available ways to construct beans via injection of beans from the store.
041 *
042 * <p>
043 * This class is instantiated through the following method:
044 * <ul class='javatree'>
045 *    <li class='jm'>{@link BeanInstantiator#of(Class)}
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
049 * <p class='bjava'>
050 *    <jc>// Bean with builder pattern</jc>
051 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
052 *       <jk>private final</jk> String <jf>value</jf>;
053 *
054 *       <jk>private</jk> MyBean(String <jv>value</jv>) {
055 *          <jk>this</jk>.<jf>value</jf> = <jv>value</jv>;
056 *       }
057 *
058 *       <jk>public static</jk> Builder <jsm>create</jsm>() {
059 *          <jk>return new</jk> Builder();
060 *       }
061 *
062 *       <jk>public static class</jk> Builder {
063 *          <jk>private</jk> String <jf>value</jf>;
064 *
065 *          <jk>public</jk> Builder value(String <jv>value</jv>) {
066 *             <jk>this</jk>.<jf>value</jf> = <jv>value</jv>;
067 *             <jk>return this</jk>;
068 *          }
069 *
070 *          <jk>public</jk> MyBean build() {
071 *             <jk>return new</jk> MyBean(<jf>value</jf>);
072 *          }
073 *       }
074 *
075 *    <jc>// Builder with @Inject on build() method</jc>
076 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean2 {
077 *       <jk>private final</jk> String <jf>value</jf>;
078 *       <jk>private final</jk> MyDependency <jf>dependency</jf>;
079 *
080 *       <jk>private</jk> MyBean2(String <jv>value</jv>, MyDependency <jv>dependency</jv>) {
081 *          <jk>this</jk>.<jf>value</jf> = <jv>value</jv>;
082 *          <jk>this</jk>.<jf>dependency</jf> = <jv>dependency</jv>;
083 *       }
084 *
085 *       <jk>public static class</jk> Builder {
086 *          <jk>private</jk> String <jf>value</jf>;
087 *
088 *          <jk>public</jk> Builder value(String <jv>value</jv>) {
089 *             <jk>this</jk>.<jf>value</jf> = <jv>value</jv>;
090 *             <jk>return this</jk>;
091 *          }
092 *
093 *          <ja>@Inject</ja>
094 *          <jk>public</jk> MyBean2 build(MyDependency <jv>dependency</jv>) {
095 *             <jk>return new</jk> MyBean2(<jv>value</jv>, <jv>dependency</jv>);
096 *          }
097 *       }
098 *    }
099 *    }
100 *
101 *    <jc>// Create bean using BeanInstantiator</jc>
102 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
103 *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
104 *       .run();
105 * </p>
106 *
107 * <h5 class='section'>Bean Creation Order:</h5>
108 * <p>
109 * The creator attempts to instantiate beans in the following order:
110 * <ol class='spaced-list'>
111 *    <li><b>Using a Builder</b> (if a builder is found or specified):
112 *       <ol>
113 *          <li>Calls a <c>build()</c>/<c>create()</c>/<c>get()</c> method on the builder that returns the bean type.
114 *             <ul>
115 *                <li>The method is <b>required</b> - if no such method exists, bean creation fails.
116 *                <li>If the method has <c>@Inject</c> or <c>@Autowired</c> annotation, its parameters are resolved from the bean store.
117 *                <li>If the method does not have <c>@Inject</c> annotation, it must have no parameters.
118 *             </ul>
119 *       </ol>
120 *    <li><b>Without a Builder</b>:
121 *       <ol>
122 *          <li>Calls a static no-arg <c>getInstance()</c> method on the bean class.
123 *          <li>Calls a public constructor with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
124 *          <li>Falls back to a protected constructor with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
125 *          <li>Falls back to a package-private constructor with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
126 *             Private constructors are deliberately excluded.
127 *       </ol>
128 * </ol>
129 *
130 * <h5 class='section'>Builder Detection:</h5>
131 * <p>
132 * Builders are detected in the following priority order:
133 * <ol class='spaced-list'>
134 *    <li>Explicitly set via {@link Builder#builder(Object)} or {@link Builder#builder(Class)}.
135 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.Builder @Builder} annotation on the bean subtype.
136 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.Builder @Builder} annotation on the bean type.
137 *    <li>Auto-detection:
138 *       <ul>
139 *          <li>Static <c>create()</c> or <c>builder()</c> method that returns a builder type
140 *             (method names can be customized via {@link Builder#builderMethodNames(String...)}).
141 *          <li>Inner class named <c>Builder</c>.
142 *       </ul>
143 * </ol>
144 *
145 * <h5 class='section'>Dependency Injection:</h5>
146 * <p>
147 * Both builder instances and the final bean have automatic dependency injection performed via
148 * {@link ClassInfo#inject(Object, BeanStore)}, which processes {@code @Inject} and {@code @Autowired} annotations
149 * on fields and methods.
150 *
151 * <p>
152 * This means that after a bean is created, all of its fields and methods annotated with {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired}
153 * will automatically be populated with beans from the bean store.
154 *
155 * <h5 class='section'>Caching:</h5>
156 * <p>
157 * By default, each call to {@link #run()} creates a new bean instance. To enable caching, call {@link Builder#cached()}.
158 * When caching is enabled, bean instances are cached using {@link Memoizer}, so multiple calls to {@link #run()}
159 * will return the same instance unless {@link #reset()} is called or configuration changes. Explicit implementations set via
160 * {@link Builder#impl(Object)} and explicit builder instances set via {@link Builder#builder(Object)} are preserved
161 * across resets.
162 *
163 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
164 * <p>
165 * Configuration is performed on the inner {@link Builder} which is <b>not</b> thread-safe. Configure on a single
166 * thread, then call {@link Builder#build()} to produce an immutable {@link BeanInstantiator} that is safe for
167 * shared concurrent use. The {@link Builder#run()} shorthand is equivalent to {@code build().run()} and is
168 * appropriate for one-shot bean creation from a single thread.
169 *
170 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
171 *    <li class='note'>Builders must have a <c>build()</c>, <c>create()</c>, or <c>get()</c> method that returns the bean type.
172 *       This method is required - constructors accepting builders are no longer supported.
173 *    <li class='note'>The builder's <c>build()</c>/<c>create()</c>/<c>get()</c> method may have <c>@Inject</c> or <c>@Autowired</c>
174 *       annotation to allow dependency injection of parameters from the bean store.
175 *    <li class='note'>If the builder's <c>build()</c>/<c>create()</c>/<c>get()</c> method does not have <c>@Inject</c> annotation,
176 *       it must have no parameters.
177 *    <li class='note'>All constructors and methods except {@link Optional} and {@link List} parameters
178 *       must have beans available in the store.
179 *    <li class='note'>If multiple constructors/methods are found, the one with the most matching parameters is used.
180 *    <li class='note'>Deprecated and <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/commons/bean/BeanIgnore.html">@BeanIgnore</a>-annotated methods/constructors are ignored.
181 * </ul>
182 *
183 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
184 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BasicBeanStore}
185 * </ul>
186 *
187 * @param <T> The bean type being created.
188 */
189@SuppressWarnings({
190   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
191   "resource" // transient build-time scratch store; lifetime is bounded by the Builder itself, no foreign resources are captured
192})
193public class BeanInstantiator<T> {
194
195   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
196   private static final String ARG_beanType = "beanType";
197   private static final String ARG_fallback = "fallback";
198   private static final String ARG_hook = "hook";
199   private static final String ARG_names = "names";
200   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
201
202   private static final String CONST_usingFallbackSupplier = "Using fallback supplier";
203
204   /** Default factory method names used for bean instantiation. */
205   protected static final Set<String> DEFAULT_FACTORY_METHOD_NAMES = u(set("getInstance"));
206
207   /** Default builder method names used for builder factory methods (static methods that create/return a builder). */
208   protected static final Set<String> DEFAULT_BUILDER_METHOD_NAMES = u(set("create", "builder"));
209
210   /** Default build method names used for builder build methods (instance methods on builder that build/return the bean). */
211   protected static final Set<String> DEFAULT_BUILD_METHOD_NAMES = u(set("build", "create", "get"));
212
213   /** Default builder class names for auto-detection. */
214   protected static final Set<String> DEFAULT_BUILDER_CLASS_NAMES = u(set("Builder"));
215
216   private static final Logger logger = Logger.getLogger(BeanInstantiator.class);
217
218   /**
219    * Lifecycle scope for the bean being created.
220    *
221    * <p>
222    * Maps to {@link Builder#cached()} so that {@link Builder#scope(Scope)} provides a more declarative alternative.
223    *
224    * @see Builder#scope(Scope)
225    */
226   public enum Scope {
227      /** A new bean instance is produced on every call to {@link Builder#run()}. */
228      PROTOTYPE,
229      /** A single bean instance is created and reused for the lifetime of the builder. */
230      SINGLETON
231   }
232
233   /**
234    * Creates a new bean creator builder.
235    *
236    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
237    * @param beanType The bean type to create.
238    * @return A new bean creator builder.
239    */
240   public static <T> Builder<T> of(Class<T> beanType) {
241      return new Builder<>(beanType, null, null, null);
242   }
243
244   /**
245    * Creates a new bean creator builder with a parent bean store.
246    *
247    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
248    * @param beanType The bean type to create.
249    * @param parentStore The parent bean store to use for resolving dependencies. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
250    * @return A new bean creator builder.
251    */
252   public static <T> Builder<T> of(Class<T> beanType, BeanStore parentStore) {
253      return new Builder<>(beanType, parentStore, null, null);
254   }
255
256   /**
257    * Creates a new bean creator builder with a parent bean store, name, and enclosing instance.
258    *
259    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
260    * @param beanType The bean type to create.
261    * @param parentStore The parent bean store to use for resolving dependencies. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
262    * @param name The bean name. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
263    * @param enclosingInstance The enclosing instance object. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
264    * @return A new bean creator builder.
265    */
266   public static <T> Builder<T> of(Class<T> beanType, BeanStore parentStore, String name, Object enclosingInstance) {
267      return new Builder<>(beanType, parentStore, name, enclosingInstance);
268   }
269
270   /**
271    * Instantiates a bean of the given type, or returns <jk>null</jk> if the type is <jk>null</jk> or instantiation fails.
272    *
273    * <p>
274    * Shorthand for the common pattern:
275    * <p class='bjava'>
276    *    <jv>cls</jv> == <jk>null</jk> ? <jk>null</jk> : BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>cls</jv>).fallback(() -> <jk>null</jk>).run()
277    * </p>
278    *
279    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
280    * @param beanType The bean type to create.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
281    * @return A new bean instance, or <jk>null</jk> if the type is <jk>null</jk> or instantiation fails.
282    */
283   public static <T> T createOrNull(Class<T> beanType) {
284      return beanType == null ? null : of(beanType).fallback(() -> null).run();
285   }
286
287   /**
288    * Instantiates a bean of the given type and wraps it in an {@link Optional}, or returns {@link Optional#empty()}
289    * if the type is <jk>null</jk> or instantiation fails.
290    *
291    * <p>
292    * Shorthand for the common pattern:
293    * <p class='bjava'>
294    *    <jv>cls</jv> == <jk>null</jk> ? Optional.empty() : BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>cls</jv>).asOptional()
295    * </p>
296    *
297    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
298    * @param beanType The bean type to create.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
299    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the created bean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if the type is <jk>null</jk> or instantiation fails.
300    */
301   public static <T> Optional<T> optionalOf(Class<T> beanType) {
302      return beanType == null ? opte() : of(beanType).asOptional();
303   }
304
305   /**
306    * Instantiates a bean of the given type, or returns a default value if the type is <jk>null</jk>.
307    *
308    * <p>
309    * Shorthand for the common pattern:
310    * <p class='bjava'>
311    *    <jv>cls</jv> == <jk>null</jk> ? <jv>defaultValue</jv> : BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>cls</jv>).run()
312    * </p>
313    *
314    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
315    * @param beanType The bean type to create.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
316    * @param defaultValue The value to return if {@code beanType} is <jk>null</jk>.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
317    * @return A new bean instance, or {@code defaultValue} if the type is <jk>null</jk>.
318    */
319   public static <T> T createOrDefault(Class<T> beanType, T defaultValue) {
320      return beanType == null ? defaultValue : of(beanType).run();
321   }
322
323   // =========================================================================
324   // Outer (immutable) wrapper state and methods
325   // =========================================================================
326
327   private final Builder<T> b;
328
329   BeanInstantiator(Builder<T> b) {
330      this.b = b;
331   }
332
333   /**
334    * Creates the bean.
335    *
336    * <p>
337    * After creating the bean, this method automatically injects dependencies into fields and methods
338    * annotated with {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} using {@link ClassInfo#inject(Object, BeanStore)}.
339    *
340    * @return A new bean with all dependencies injected.
341    * @throws ExecutableException if bean could not be created.
342    */
343   public T run() {
344      return b.runImpl();
345   }
346
347   /**
348    * Attempts to create the bean, returning an {@link Optional} instead of throwing an exception on failure.
349    *
350    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the created bean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if creation failed.
351    */
352   public Optional<T> asOptional() {
353      try {
354         return opt(run());
355      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") ExecutableException e) {
356         return opte();
357      }
358   }
359
360   /**
361    * Converts this creator into a memoizer.
362    *
363    * @return A resettable supplier that caches the created bean until reset.
364    */
365   public Memoizer<T> asMemoizer() {
366      return new Memoizer<>(this::run);
367   }
368
369   /**
370    * Converts this creator into a supplier.
371    *
372    * @return A supplier that returns the results of the {@link #run()} method.
373    */
374   public Supplier<T> asSupplier() {
375      return this::run;
376   }
377
378   /**
379    * Resets the cached bean instance and the inner Builder's resolved memoizers.
380    *
381    * @return This object.
382    */
383   public BeanInstantiator<T> reset() {
384      b.reset();
385      return this;
386   }
387
388   /**
389    * Returns the name of the bean being created.
390    *
391    * @return The bean name, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
392    */
393   public String getName() {
394      return b.name;
395   }
396
397   /**
398    * Returns the bean subtype class info if specified, or the normal bean type if not.
399    *
400    * @return The bean subtype class info, or the bean type class info if no subtype was specified.
401    */
402   protected ClassInfo getBeanSubType() {
403      return b.beanSubType;
404   }
405
406   /**
407    * Returns the list of bean subtype classes, including the bean subtype, bean type, and all classes in between.
408    *
409    * @return A list of bean subtype class info objects, never <jk>null</jk>.
410    */
411   protected List<ClassInfo> getBeanSubTypes() {
412      return b.getBeanSubTypes();
413   }
414
415   /**
416    * Returns the builder instance if one has been created or specified.
417    *
418    * @param <B> The builder type.
419    * @return The builder instance wrapped in an {@link Optional}, or an empty optional if no builder exists.
420    */
421   public <B> Optional<B> getBuilder() {
422      return b.getBuilder();
423   }
424
425   /**
426    * Returns the builder type class info determined by builder type detection.
427    *
428    * @return The builder type class info, or <jk>null</jk> if no builder type was determined.
429    */
430   protected ClassInfo getBuilderType() {
431      return b.getBuilderType();
432   }
433
434   /**
435    * Returns the list of builder types, including the primary builder type and any builder types found in the builder's parent hierarchy.
436    *
437    * @return A list of builder type class info objects, never <jk>null</jk>.
438    */
439   protected List<ClassInfo> getBuilderTypes() {
440      return b.getBuilderTypes();
441   }
442
443   /**
444    * Returns an unmodifiable list of debug log entries from the last bean creation attempt.
445    *
446    * @return An unmodifiable list of debug log entries.
447    */
448   public List<String> getDebugLog() {
449      return b.getDebugLog();
450   }
451
452   // =========================================================================
453   // Builder (mutable, single-thread configuration) inner class
454   // =========================================================================
455
456   /**
457    * Builder for {@link BeanInstantiator}.
458    *
459    * <p>
460    * Holds all mutable configuration. Configure on a single thread, then call {@link #build()} to obtain
461    * an immutable {@link BeanInstantiator} suitable for shared concurrent use. Most call sites use the
462    * {@link #run()} shorthand which is equivalent to {@code build().run()}.
463    *
464    * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
465    * <p>
466    * Builder instances are <b>not</b> thread-safe. Configure on one thread, then either call {@link #build()}
467    * to produce a thread-safe immutable {@link BeanInstantiator}, or call {@link #run()} to one-shot create a
468    * bean from a single thread.
469    *
470    * @param <T> The bean type being created.
471    */
472   public static class Builder<T> {
473
474   final BeanStore parentStore;
475   final BasicBeanStore store;
476   final ClassInfoTyped<T> beanType;
477   final NullableReference<List<String>> debug = NullableReference.empty();
478
479   private ClassInfoTyped<? extends T> beanSubType;
480   private ClassInfo explicitBuilderType = null;
481   private Object explicitBuilder = null;
482   private final Object enclosingInstance;
483   private T explicitImplementation = null;
484   private List<Consumer<T>> postCreateHooks = new ArrayList<>();
485   private Set<String> factoryMethodNames = DEFAULT_FACTORY_METHOD_NAMES;
486   private Set<String> builderMethodNames = DEFAULT_BUILDER_METHOD_NAMES;
487   private Set<String> buildMethodNames = DEFAULT_BUILD_METHOD_NAMES;
488   private Set<String> builderClassNames = DEFAULT_BUILDER_CLASS_NAMES;
489   private Supplier<? extends T> fallbackSupplier = null;
490   private final String name;
491   private boolean cached = false;
492   private boolean factoryAbstainOnNull = false;
493   private boolean preferZeroArgConstructor = false;
494   private boolean silent = false;
495   private String description = null;
496   private List<UnaryOperator<T>> wrappers = new ArrayList<>();
497   private List<Validation<T>> validators = new ArrayList<>();
498   private List<Builder<? extends T>> alternatives = new ArrayList<>();
499   private List<Consumer<Object>> builderInitializers = new ArrayList<>();
500   private boolean injectBuilder = false;
501   private boolean autoWireBuilder = false;
502   private boolean autoWireUnwrapSuppliers = true;
503   private boolean noBuilder = false;
504
505   private Memoizer<ClassInfo> builderType = memoizer(() -> findBuilderType());
506   private Memoizer<List<ClassInfo>> builderTypes = memoizer(() -> findBuilderTypes());
507   private Memoizer<List<ClassInfo>> beanSubTypes = memoizer(() -> findBeanSubTypes());
508   private Memoizer<Object> builderMemoizer = memoizer(() -> findBuilder());
509   private Memoizer<T> beanImpl = memoizer(() -> processBean(findBeanImpl()));
510
511   /**
512    * Constructor.
513    *
514    * @param beanType The bean type being created.
515    * @param parentStore The parent bean store to use for resolving dependencies. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
516    * @param name The bean name. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
517    * @param enclosingInstance The enclosing instance object. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
518    */
519   protected Builder(Class<T> beanType, BeanStore parentStore, String name, Object enclosingInstance) {
520      this.beanType = info(assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanType, beanType));
521      this.beanSubType = this.beanType;
522      this.parentStore = parentStore;
523      this.store = new BasicBeanStore(this.parentStore);
524      this.name = name;
525      this.enclosingInstance = enclosingInstance;
526   }
527
528   /**
529    * Adds a bean to the internal bean store and returns it.
530    *
531    * @param <T2> The bean type.
532    * @param type The bean type.
533    * @param bean The bean instance.
534    * @return The bean that was added.
535    */
536   public <T2> T2 add(Class<T2> type, T2 bean) {
537      store.add(type, bean);
538      reset();
539      return bean;
540   }
541
542   /**
543    * Adds a bean to the internal bean store.
544    *
545    * @param <T2> The bean type.
546    * @param type The bean type.
547    * @param bean The bean instance.
548    * @return This object.
549    */
550   public <T2> Builder<T> addBean(Class<T2> type, T2 bean) {
551      store.add(type, bean);
552      reset();
553      return this;
554   }
555
556   /**
557    * Adds a named bean to the internal bean store.
558    *
559    * @param <T2> The bean type.
560    * @param type The bean type.
561    * @param bean The bean instance.
562    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
563    * @return This object.
564    */
565   public <T2> Builder<T> addBean(Class<T2> type, T2 bean, String name) {
566      store.add(type, bean, name);
567      reset();
568      return this;
569   }
570
571   /**
572    * Attempts to create the bean, returning an {@link Optional} instead of throwing an exception on failure.
573    *
574    * <p>
575    * This is a non-throwing variant of {@link #run()} that wraps the result in an {@link Optional}.
576    * If bean creation fails for any reason (missing dependencies, abstract class, interface, no matching constructor, etc.),
577    * this method returns {@link Optional#empty()} instead of throwing {@link ExecutableException}.
578    *
579    * <p>
580    * This method is useful when bean creation failure is expected and should not be treated as exceptional,
581    * such as when implementing optional features or trying multiple creation strategies.
582    *
583    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
584    *    <li class='note'>Unlike {@link #run()}, this method catches {@link ExecutableException} and returns empty.
585    *    <li class='note'>If a fallback is registered via {@link #fallback(Supplier)},
586    *       it will be used before returning empty, so this method will only return empty if both
587    *       normal creation AND fallback fail.
588    *    <li class='note'>The created bean is cached via {@link Memoizer}, so subsequent calls return the same instance
589    *       unless {@link #reset()} is called or configuration changes.
590    *    <li class='note'>Post-creation hooks are still executed if creation succeeds.
591    *    <li class='note'>Other exceptions (e.g., from post-creation hooks or fallback suppliers) are NOT caught
592    *       and will propagate to the caller.
593    * </ul>
594    *
595    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
596    * <p class='bjava'>
597    *    <jc>// Try to create, use default if it fails</jc>
598    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
599    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
600    *       .asOptional()
601    *       .orElse(<jk>new</jk> DefaultMyBean());
602    *
603    *    <jc>// Chain multiple creation attempts</jc>
604    *    MyService <jv>service</jv> = BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(AdvancedService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
605    *       .asOptional()
606    *       .or(() -&gt; BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(BasicService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
607    *          .asOptional())
608    *       .orElseGet(() -&gt; <jk>new</jk> FallbackService());
609    *
610    *    <jc>// Check if optional feature is available</jc>
611    *    Optional&lt;OptionalFeature&gt; <jv>feature</jv> = BeanInstantiator
612    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(OptionalFeature.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
613    *       .asOptional();
614    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>feature</jv>.isPresent()) {
615    *       <jv>feature</jv>.get().enable();
616    *    }
617    * </p>
618    *
619    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the created bean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if creation failed.
620    */
621   public Optional<T> asOptional() {
622      return build().asOptional();
623   }
624
625   /**
626    * Converts this creator into a memoizer.
627    *
628    * <p>
629    * A {@link Memoizer} caches the result of the first {@link Memoizer#get()} call
630    * and returns that cached value for subsequent calls. The {@link Memoizer#reset()} method
631    * can be used to clear the cache, forcing the bean to be recreated on the next get() call.
632    *
633    * <p>
634    * This is particularly useful when the bean store contents change and you want to recreate the bean
635    * with updated dependencies.
636    *
637    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
638    *    <li class='note'>The returned {@link Memoizer} is thread-safe.
639    *    <li class='note'>Each call to {@link Memoizer#get()} after a reset() will invoke
640    *       {@link #run()}, which may trigger dependency injection and post-creation hooks.
641    *    <li class='note'>The memoizer inherits all Optional-like methods from {@link NullableSupplier}.
642    * </ul>
643    *
644    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
645    * <p class='bjava'>
646    *    <jc>// Create a memoizer for a bean with dependencies</jc>
647    *    BeanStore <jv>store</jv> = <jk>new</jk> BasicBeanStore(<jk>null</jk>);
648    *    <jv>store</jv>.addBean(String.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"initial"</js>);
649    *
650    *    Memoizer&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>supplier</jv> = BeanInstantiator
651    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
652    *       .asMemoizer();
653    *
654    *    <jc>// First call creates and caches the bean</jc>
655    *    MyBean <jv>bean1</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.get();
656    *
657    *    <jc>// Subsequent calls return the cached bean</jc>
658    *    MyBean <jv>bean2</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.get();
659    *    <jsm>assertSame</jsm>(<jv>bean1</jv>, <jv>bean2</jv>);
660    *
661    *    <jc>// Update the bean store</jc>
662    *    <jv>store</jv>.addBean(String.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"updated"</js>);
663    *
664    *    <jc>// Reset forces recreation with updated dependencies</jc>
665    *    <jv>supplier</jv>.reset();
666    *    MyBean <jv>bean3</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.get();
667    *    <jsm>assertNotSame</jsm>(<jv>bean1</jv>, <jv>bean3</jv>);  <jc>// New instance with updated deps</jc>
668    * </p>
669    *
670    * @return A resettable supplier that caches the created bean until reset.
671    */
672   public Memoizer<T> asMemoizer() {
673      return build().asMemoizer();
674   }
675
676   /**
677    * Converts this creator into a supplier.
678    *
679    * @return A supplier that returns the results of the {@link #run()} method.
680    */
681   public Supplier<T> asSupplier() {
682      return build().asSupplier();
683   }
684
685   /**
686    * Specifies a subclass of the bean type to create.
687    *
688    * <p>
689    * This allows you to create a specific implementation when the bean type is an interface, abstract class,
690    * or when you want to instantiate a customized subclass of a concrete class.
691    *
692    * @param value The subclass type to create. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>. Must be a subclass of {@code beanType}.
693    * @return This object.
694    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If value is not a subclass of {@code beanType}.
695    */
696   public Builder<T> type(Class<? extends T> value) {
697      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
698      beanSubType = info(value);
699      assertArg(beanType.isAssignableFrom(beanSubType), "type must be a subclass of beanType. beanType={0}, type={1}", cn(beanType), cn(beanSubType));
700      reset();
701      return this;
702   }
703
704   /**
705    * Specifies a subclass of the bean type to create, via a {@link ClassInfo} reference.
706    *
707    * <p>
708    * Convenience overload of {@link #type(Class)} that accepts {@link ClassInfo} (or any subtype such
709    * as {@code ClassMeta}).  The underlying class is extracted via {@link ClassInfo#inner()} and the
710    * subtype-relationship check happens at runtime.
711    *
712    * @param value The subclass type to create.  Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
713    * @return This object.
714    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or its underlying class is not a subclass of {@code beanType}.
715    */
716   @SuppressWarnings({
717      "unchecked" // Reflective builder lookup returns Object; cast is safe by construction.
718   })
719   public Builder<T> type(ClassInfo value) {
720      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
721      return type((Class<? extends T>) value.inner());
722   }
723
724   /**
725    * Specifies the builder type to use for creating the bean.
726    *
727    * <p>
728    * This method triggers builder type detection and instantiation for the specified type.
729    *
730    * <p>
731    * The builder type must be valid, meaning it must have a <c>build()</c>, <c>create()</c>, or <c>get()</c> method
732    * that returns the bean type (or a parent of the bean type). The method may have <c>@Inject</c> or <c>@Autowired</c>
733    * annotation to allow injected parameters; otherwise, it must have no parameters.
734    *
735    * <p>
736    * If the builder type is invalid (does not have a valid build method), this method throws an
737    * {@link IllegalArgumentException}.
738    *
739 * <p>
740 * The builder will be instantiated by looking for:
741 * <ol>
742 *    <li>A static <c>create()</c> or <c>builder()</c> method that returns the builder type.
743 *    <li>A public constructor on the builder type with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
744 *    <li>A protected constructor on the builder type with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
745 *    <li>A package-private constructor on the builder type with parameters that can be resolved from the bean store.
746 *       Private constructors are deliberately excluded.
747 * </ol>
748    *
749    * @param value The builder type. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
750    * @return This object.
751    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the builder type is invalid (does not have a valid build/create/get method).
752    */
753   public Builder<T> builder(Class<?> value) {
754      explicitBuilderType = info(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
755      builderType.set(explicitBuilderType);
756      assertArg(isValidBuilderType(explicitBuilderType), "Invalid builder type {0} for bean type {1}. Builder must have a build(), create(), or get() method that returns {1} (or a parent of {1}). The method may have @Inject annotation to allow injected parameters; otherwise, it must have no parameters.", cn(explicitBuilderType), cn(beanType));
757      builderTypes.get();  // Triggers validation on type hierarchy.
758      reset();
759      return this;
760   }
761
762   /**
763    * Specifies an existing builder instance to use for creating the bean.
764    *
765    * <p>
766    * The builder instance's class must be a valid builder type, meaning it must have a <c>build()</c>, <c>create()</c>,
767    * or <c>get()</c> method that returns the bean type (or a parent of the bean type). The method may have
768    * <c>@Inject</c> or <c>@Autowired</c> annotation to allow injected parameters; otherwise, it must have no parameters.
769    *
770    * <p>
771    * If the builder instance's class is invalid (does not have a valid build method), this method throws an
772    * {@link IllegalArgumentException}.
773    *
774    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
775    *    <li class='note'>When specified, the builder type detection is bypassed.
776    *    <li class='note'>The builder will be used to create the bean via:
777    *       <ul>
778    *          <li>A <c>build()</c>/<c>create()</c>/<c>get()</c> method on the builder (required).
779    *             The method may have <c>@Inject</c> annotation to allow injected parameters.
780    *       </ul>
781    *    <li class='note'>The explicit builder instance is preserved even when {@link #reset()} is called,
782    *       unlike cached results from suppliers.
783    * </ul>
784    *
785    * @param value The builder instance. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
786    * @return This object.
787    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the builder instance's class is invalid (does not have a valid build/create/get method).
788    */
789   public Builder<T> builder(Object value) {
790      builder(value.getClass());
791      explicitBuilder = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
792      reset();
793      return this;
794   }
795
796   /**
797    * Specifies custom class names to look for when auto-detecting builder inner classes.
798    *
799    * <p>
800    * By default, {@code BeanInstantiator} looks for inner classes named {@code "Builder"}.
801    * This method allows you to specify alternative class names.
802    *
803    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
804    *    <li class='note'>Inner builder classes must be public, static, and pass builder validation
805    *       (have a build/create/get method or be accepted by a bean constructor).
806    *    <li class='note'>If multiple inner classes match, the first valid one found will be used.
807    *    <li class='note'>This only affects inner class detection. It does not affect builders specified via
808    *       {@link #builder(Class)}, {@link #builder(Object)}, or the {@link Builder @Builder} annotation.
809    *    <li class='note'>Calling this method replaces the default builder class names.
810    * </ul>
811    *
812    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
813    * <p class='bjava'>
814    *    <jc>// Support alternative builder class naming conventions</jc>
815    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
816    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
817    *       .builderClassNames(<js>"BuilderImpl"</js>, <js>"Factory"</js>)
818    *       .run();
819    * </p>
820    *
821    * @param names The builder class names to look for. Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> elements.
822    * @return This object.
823    */
824   public Builder<T> builderClassNames(String... names) {
825      builderClassNames = set(assertArgNoNulls(ARG_names, names));
826      reset();
827      return this;
828   }
829
830   /**
831    * Specifies custom method names to look for when auto-detecting builder factory methods.
832    *
833    * <p>
834    * By default, {@code BeanInstantiator} looks for static methods named {@code "create"} or {@code "builder"}
835    * that return a builder type. This method allows you to specify alternative method names.
836    *
837    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
838    *    <li class='note'>Builder factory methods must be public, static, non-deprecated, and return a builder type
839    *       (not the bean type itself).
840    *    <li class='note'>If multiple builder factory methods match, the one with the most parameters that can be
841    *       resolved from the bean store will be used.
842    *    <li class='note'>This affects both builder type detection and builder instance creation.
843    *    <li class='note'>Calling this method replaces the default builder method names.
844    * </ul>
845    *
846    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
847    * <p class='bjava'>
848    *    <jc>// Support alternative builder factory method naming conventions</jc>
849    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
850    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
851    *       .builderMethodNames(<js>"newBuilder"</js>, <js>"instance"</js>)
852      .run();
853    * </p>
854    *
855    * @param names The builder factory method names to look for. Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> elements.
856    * @return This object.
857    */
858   public Builder<T> builderMethodNames(String... names) {
859      builderMethodNames = set(assertArgNoNulls(ARG_names, names));
860      reset();
861      return this;
862   }
863
864   /**
865    * Specifies custom method names to look for when calling build methods on builder instances.
866    *
867    * <p>
868    * By default, {@code BeanInstantiator} looks for instance methods named {@code "build"}, {@code "create"}, or {@code "get"}
869    * on the builder that return the bean type. This method allows you to specify alternative method names.
870    *
871    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
872    *    <li class='note'>Build methods must be public, non-static, non-deprecated, and return the bean type
873    *       (or a parent type of the bean type).
874    *    <li class='note'>If the method has {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} annotation, its parameters are resolved from the bean store.
875    *    <li class='note'>If the method does not have {@code @Inject} annotation, it must have no parameters.
876    *    <li class='note'>Calling this method replaces the default build method names.
877    * </ul>
878    *
879    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
880    * <p class='bjava'>
881    *    <jc>// Support alternative build method naming conventions</jc>
882    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
883    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
884    *       .buildMethodNames(<js>"execute"</js>, <js>"make"</js>)
885      .run();
886    * </p>
887    *
888    * @param names The build method names to look for. Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> elements.
889    * @return This object.
890    */
891   public Builder<T> buildMethodNames(String... names) {
892      buildMethodNames = set(assertArgNoNulls(ARG_names, names));
893      reset();
894      return this;
895   }
896
897   /**
898    * Enables caching mode.
899    *
900    * <p>
901    * When caching mode is enabled, bean instances are cached using {@link Memoizer},
902    * so multiple calls to {@link #run()} will return the same instance unless {@link #reset()} is called
903    * or configuration changes.
904    *
905    * <p>
906    * When caching mode is disabled (the default), each call to {@link #run()} will create a new bean instance
907    * by calling {@link #findBeanImpl()} directly.
908    *
909    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
910    * <p class='bjava'>
911    *    <jc>// Enable caching mode - same instance returned on each run()</jc>
912    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>creator</jv> = BeanInstantiator
913    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
914    *       .<jsm>cached</jsm>();
915    *    MyBean <jv>bean1</jv> = <jv>creator</jv>.<jsm>run</jsm>();
916    *    MyBean <jv>bean2</jv> = <jv>creator</jv>.<jsm>run</jsm>();
917    *    <jc>// bean1 and bean2 are the same instance</jc>
918    * </p>
919    *
920    * @return This object.
921    */
922   public Builder<T> cached() {
923      cached = true;
924      return this;
925   }
926
927   /**
928    * Treat a {@code null} return value from a static factory method as a deliberate "abstain" signal.
929    *
930    * <p>
931    * By default, when a static factory method (one of {@link #factoryMethodNames(String...)}) matches and is
932    * invoked, but returns {@code null}, {@link #run()} falls through to constructor lookup as if the factory
933    * method weren't there. This works well when the factory method is just an alternate construction path.
934    *
935    * <p>
936    * Some factory-method patterns instead use a {@code null} return value as a deliberate "this implementation
937    * does not handle the input — try the next strategy" signal, expecting the caller to interpret the {@code null}
938    * as a definitive answer. The classic example is a {@code RestOpArg} subclass whose
939    * {@code static create(ParameterInfo)} returns {@code null} when the parameter isn't annotated with the
940    * marker the subclass handles, so the caller can move on to the next subclass in the chain. Falling through
941    * to the (always-present) constructor in that case would silently materialize an arg that doesn't apply.
942    *
943    * <p>
944    * When this flag is enabled, a {@code null} return from the matching factory method is propagated out of
945    * {@link #run()} unchanged — constructor lookup is skipped. This matches legacy {@code BeanCreator}
946    * semantics for static {@code create()} / {@code builder()} / {@code getInstance()} methods.
947    *
948    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
949    * <p class='bjava'>
950    *    <jc>// AttributeArg.create(ParameterInfo) returns null if @Attr is not present.</jc>
951    *    <jc>// Without factoryAbstainOnNull, BeanInstantiator would then invoke the AttributeArg(ParameterInfo)</jc>
952    *    <jc>// constructor and return a bogus instance.</jc>
953    *    RestOpArg <jv>arg</jv> = BeanInstantiator
954    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(RestOpArg.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
955    *       .type(AttributeArg.<jk>class</jk>)
956    *       .factoryMethodNames(<js>"getInstance"</js>, <js>"create"</js>)
957    *       .factoryAbstainOnNull()
958    *       .run();
959    * </p>
960    *
961    * @return This object.
962    */
963   public Builder<T> factoryAbstainOnNull() {
964      factoryAbstainOnNull = true;
965      return this;
966   }
967
968   /**
969    * Prefer a zero-argument constructor when one exists, instead of injecting via the longest constructor.
970    *
971    * <p>
972    * By default, {@code BeanInstantiator} sorts public constructors by parameter count <i>descending</i>: the
973    * constructor with the most resolvable parameters wins, encouraging full constructor injection. This is the
974    * right default for most beans, but it doesn't fit every use case.
975    *
976    * <p>
977    * The classic mismatch is parameterized container types like {@link java.util.TreeMap}, which expose:
978    * <ul>
979    *    <li>{@code TreeMap()}
980    *    <li>{@code TreeMap(Comparator)}
981    *    <li>{@code TreeMap(Map<? extends K, ? extends V>)}
982    *    <li>{@code TreeMap(SortedMap)}
983    * </ul>
984    * The default selection picks the {@code Map} copy-constructor — but that {@code Map<? extends K, ? extends V>}
985    * parameter doesn't satisfy v2 inject-collection auto-resolution (which only handles {@code Map<String, T>}),
986    * so injection fails at runtime even though {@link ParameterInfo#canResolve(BeanStore, Object...)} optimistically
987    * said yes. Callers that just want "give me a fresh empty container" should use this flag.
988    *
989    * <p>
990    * When this flag is enabled, if {@code beanSubType} declares a public no-arg constructor, that constructor is
991    * used unconditionally and other constructors are ignored. This matches legacy {@code BeanCreator} behavior for
992    * the same use case (legacy filtered out the multi-arg ctors via {@code hasAllParams} returning false, since the
993    * legacy bean store didn't auto-resolve raw {@code Map}/{@code Collection} parameter types).
994    *
995    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
996    * <p class='bjava'>
997    *    <jc>// Instantiating an arbitrary Map subclass — prefer the no-arg ctor over copy-ctors.</jc>
998    *    Map&lt;?,?&gt; <jv>m</jv> = BeanInstantiator
999    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(Map.<jk>class</jk>)
1000    *       .type(treeMapClass)
1001    *       .preferZeroArgConstructor()
1002    *       .run();
1003    * </p>
1004    *
1005    * @return This object.
1006    */
1007   public Builder<T> preferZeroArgConstructor() {
1008      preferZeroArgConstructor = true;
1009      return this;
1010   }
1011
1012   /**
1013    * Compiles this builder into a {@link BeanInstantiator}.
1014    *
1015    * <p>
1016    * The returned instance exposes only run/read methods (no setters). The compiled instance shares
1017    * its caching state with this builder so that {@link #cached()} memoization is consistent across
1018    * both compiled and shorthand call paths.
1019    *
1020    * @return A new {@link BeanInstantiator} backed by this builder's configuration.
1021    */
1022   public BeanInstantiator<T> build() {
1023      return new BeanInstantiator<>(this);
1024   }
1025
1026   /**
1027    * Shortcut for {@code build().run()}.
1028    *
1029    * <p>
1030    * Most call sites do not store the {@link BeanInstantiator} and therefore call this shortcut.
1031    * Repeated calls share caching with any compiled {@link BeanInstantiator} produced by {@link #build()}.
1032    *
1033    * @return A new bean with all dependencies injected.
1034    * @throws ExecutableException if bean could not be created.
1035    */
1036   public T run() {
1037      return runImpl();
1038   }
1039
1040   T runImpl() {
1041      debug.ifPresent(x -> x.clear());
1042
1043      try {
1044         return runImplOnce();
1045      } catch (RuntimeException e) {
1046         if (alternatives.isEmpty())
1047            throw e;
1048         for (var alt : alternatives) {
1049            try {
1050               return processBean(alt.runImpl());
1051            } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") RuntimeException ignored) {
1052               // Try next alternative
1053            }
1054         }
1055         throw e;
1056      }
1057   }
1058
1059   private T runImplOnce() {
1060      if (neq(beanSubType, beanType))
1061         log("Subtype specified: %s", beanSubType.getName());
1062
1063      if (explicitImplementation != null) {
1064         log("Using pre-configured impl() instance");
1065         return processBean(runPostCreateHooks(inject(explicitImplementation)));
1066      }
1067
1068      if (cached) {
1069         log("Using cached instance");
1070         return beanImpl.get();
1071      }
1072
1073      log("Using new instance");
1074      return processBean(findBeanImpl());
1075   }
1076
1077   private T processBean(T bean) {
1078      for (var w : wrappers)
1079         bean = w.apply(bean);
1080      for (var v : validators) {
1081         if (! v.predicate().test(bean))
1082            throw new ExecutableException(v.message() + " (bean=" + bean + ")");
1083      }
1084      return bean;
1085   }
1086
1087   /**
1088    * Enables debug mode to track the bean creation process.
1089    *
1090    * <p>
1091    * When debug mode is enabled, the creator logs each step of the bean creation process
1092    * to an internal list. This log can be retrieved via {@link #getDebugLog()} to understand
1093    * which creation path was taken, why creation might have failed, or to troubleshoot
1094    * unexpected behavior.
1095    *
1096    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1097    *    <li class='note'>Debug logs include: builder detection, factory method attempts, constructor attempts,
1098    *       dependency resolution, injection, hooks execution, validation, and fallback usage.
1099    *    <li class='note'>The debug log is reset each time {@link #run()} or {@link #asOptional()} is called.
1100    *    <li class='note'>Debug mode has minimal performance impact but should generally be disabled in production.
1101    *    <li class='note'>The debug log is stored in memory and can be retrieved after bean creation.
1102    *    <li class='note'>When caching is enabled via {@link #cached()}, the log reflects the first creation only
1103    *       (subsequent calls return the cached instance unless {@link #reset()} is called).
1104    * </ul>
1105    *
1106    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1107    * <p class='bjava'>
1108    *    <jc>// Enable debug mode</jc>
1109    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>creator</jv> = BeanInstantiator
1110    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
1111    *       .debug();
1112    *
1113    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>creator</jv>.run();
1114    *
1115    *    <jc>// Print debug log</jc>
1116    *    <jv>creator</jv>.getDebugLog().forEach(System.<jf>out</jf>::println);
1117    * </p>
1118    *
1119    * @return This object.
1120    */
1121   public Builder<T> debug() {
1122      debug.set(synchronizedList(new ArrayList<>()));
1123      return this;
1124   }
1125
1126
1127   /**
1128    * Specifies custom static factory method names to look for when creating the bean.
1129    *
1130    * <p>
1131    * By default, {@link BeanInstantiator} looks for a static {@code getInstance()} method when attempting
1132    * to create beans without a builder. This method allows you to specify alternative
1133    * factory method names to support non-standard naming conventions.
1134    *
1135    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1136    *    <li class='note'>Factory methods must be public, static, non-deprecated, and return the bean type.
1137    *    <li class='note'>If multiple factory methods match, the one with the most parameters that can be
1138    *       resolved from the bean store will be used.
1139    *    <li class='note'>Factory methods are attempted before constructors in the bean creation order.
1140    *    <li class='note'>This setting only affects factory method detection; it does not affect builder detection.
1141    *    <li class='note'>Calling this method replaces the default factory method names.
1142    * </ul>
1143    *
1144    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1145    * <p class='bjava'>
1146    *    <jc>// Support multiple factory method naming conventions</jc>
1147    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = BeanInstantiator
1148    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
1149    *       .factoryMethodNames(<js>"of"</js>, <js>"from"</js>, <js>"create"</js>, <js>"newInstance"</js>)
1150    *       .run();
1151    * </p>
1152    *
1153    * @param names The factory method names to look for. Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> elements.
1154    * @return This object.
1155    */
1156   public Builder<T> factoryMethodNames(String... names) {
1157      factoryMethodNames = set(assertArgNoNulls(ARG_names, names));
1158      reset();
1159      return this;
1160   }
1161
1162   /**
1163    * Specifies a fallback supplier to use if bean creation fails.
1164    *
1165    * <p>
1166    * When a fallback supplier is registered, if bean creation fails (throws {@link ExecutableException}),
1167    * instead of propagating the exception, the fallback supplier will be invoked to provide a default instance.
1168    *
1169    * <p>
1170    * This enables graceful degradation when bean creation cannot succeed, such as when optional features
1171    * or dependencies are not available.
1172    *
1173    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1174    *    <li class='note'>The fallback supplier is only invoked if bean creation fails.
1175    *       If bean creation succeeds normally, the fallback is never called.
1176    *    <li class='note'>Fallbacks are checked after all normal creation attempts (builders, factory methods, constructors).
1177    *    <li class='note'>The fallback-provided instance will have post-creation hooks executed on it.
1178    *    <li class='note'>If the fallback is used, that instance is cached via {@link Memoizer} and returned on subsequent calls
1179    *       unless {@link #reset()} is called or configuration changes.
1180    *    <li class='note'>Only one fallback can be registered. Subsequent calls to this method will replace
1181    *       the previous fallback.
1182    *    <li class='note'>If the fallback supplier itself throws an exception, that exception will propagate to the caller.
1183    *    <li class='note'>To use a pre-created instance as a fallback, wrap it in a supplier: <c>fallback(() -&gt; myInstance)</c>.
1184    * </ul>
1185    *
1186    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1187    * <p class='bjava'>
1188    *    <jc>// Provide a default instance if creation fails</jc>
1189    *    MyService <jv>service</jv> = BeanInstantiator
1190    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
1191    *       .fallback(() -&gt; <jk>new</jk> DefaultMyService())
1192    *       .run();
1193    *
1194    *    <jc>// Use a pre-created instance as fallback</jc>
1195    *    MyService <jv>defaultService</jv> = <jk>new</jk> DefaultMyService();
1196    *    MyService <jv>service2</jv> = BeanInstantiator
1197    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
1198    *       .fallback(() -&gt; <jv>defaultService</jv>)
1199    *       .run();
1200    * </p>
1201    *
1202    * @param fallback The fallback supplier. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1203    * @return This object.
1204    */
1205   public Builder<T> fallback(Supplier<? extends T> fallback) {
1206      assertArgNotNull(ARG_fallback, fallback);
1207      this.fallbackSupplier = fallback;
1208      return this;
1209   }
1210
1211   /**
1212    * Returns the bean subtype class info if specified, or the normal bean type if not.
1213    *
1214    * <p>
1215    * If {@link #type(Class)} was called to specify a subtype, returns that subtype.
1216    * Otherwise, returns the original bean type passed to {@link #of(Class)}.
1217    *
1218    * @return The bean subtype class info, or the bean type class info if no subtype was specified.
1219    */
1220   protected ClassInfo getBeanSubType() {
1221      return beanSubType;
1222   }
1223
1224   /**
1225    * Returns the name of the bean being created.
1226    *
1227    * <p>
1228    * Returns the name that was passed to {@link #of(Class, BeanStore, String, Object)}, or <jk>null</jk> if no name was specified.
1229    *
1230    * @return The bean name, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
1231    */
1232   public String getName() {
1233      return name;
1234   }
1235
1236   /**
1237    * Returns the list of bean subtype classes, including the bean subtype, bean type, and all classes in between.
1238    *
1239    * <p>
1240    * This method returns a list that starts with the bean subtype (if different from bean type) and includes
1241    * all parent classes up to and including the bean type. If no subtype was specified, the list will contain
1242    * only the bean type.
1243    *
1244    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1245    * <p class='bjava'>
1246    *    <jc>// If beanType is A and beanSubType is C (extends B extends A)</jc>
1247    *    <jc>// getBeanSubTypes() returns: [C, B, A]</jc>
1248    * </p>
1249    *
1250    * @return A list of bean subtype class info objects, never <jk>null</jk>.
1251    */
1252   protected List<ClassInfo> getBeanSubTypes() {
1253      return beanSubTypes.get();
1254   }
1255
1256   /**
1257    * Returns the builder instance if one has been created or specified.
1258    *
1259    * @param <B> The builder type.
1260    * @return The builder instance wrapped in an {@link Optional}, or an empty optional if no builder exists.
1261    */
1262   @SuppressWarnings({
1263      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to B for builder retrieval
1264   })
1265   public <B> Optional<B> getBuilder() {
1266      beanImpl.reset();
1267      return (Optional<B>)builderMemoizer.toOptional();
1268   }
1269
1270   /**
1271    * Returns the builder type class info determined by {@link #findBuilderType()}.
1272    *
1273    * @return The builder type class info, or <jk>null</jk> if no builder type was determined.
1274    */
1275   protected ClassInfo getBuilderType() {
1276      return builderType.get();
1277   }
1278
1279   /**
1280    * Returns the list of builder types, including the primary builder type and any builder types found in the builder's parent hierarchy.
1281    *
1282    * <p>
1283    * This method returns a list that includes the primary builder type and all builder types found by traversing
1284    * the builder's parent hierarchy. This allows parent builders to be resolved as injectable types.
1285    *
1286    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1287    * <p class='bjava'>
1288    *    <jc>// Class A with builder</jc>
1289    *    <jk>class</jk> A {
1290    *       <jk>static class</jk> Builder {
1291    *          A create() { <jk>return new</jk> A(); }
1292    *       }
1293    *    }
1294    *
1295    *    <jc>// Class B extends A with builder</jc>
1296    *    <jk>class</jk> B <jk>extends</jk> A {
1297    *       <jk>static class</jk> Builder <jk>extends</jk> A.Builder {
1298    *          B create() { <jk>return new</jk> B(); }
1299    *       }
1300    *    }
1301    *
1302    *    <jc>// When creating B, getBuilderTypes() returns: [B.Builder, A.Builder]</jc>
1303    * </p>
1304    *
1305    * @return A list of builder type class info objects, never <jk>null</jk>.
1306    */
1307   protected List<ClassInfo> getBuilderTypes() {
1308      return builderTypes.get();
1309   }
1310
1311   /**
1312    * Returns an unmodifiable list of debug log entries from the last bean creation attempt.
1313    *
1314    * <p>
1315    * This method returns the detailed log of steps taken during the most recent call to
1316    * {@link #run()} or {@link #asOptional()}. The log is only populated when {@link #debug()}
1317    * mode is enabled.
1318    *
1319    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1320    *    <li class='note'>Returns an empty list if {@link #debug()} was not called or if no creation has been attempted.
1321    *    <li class='note'>The log is reset at the beginning of each {@link #run()} call.
1322    *    <li class='note'>Since {@link #run()} uses a cached {@link Memoizer}, the log reflects only the first creation
1323    *       (subsequent calls return the cached instance unless {@link #reset()} is called).
1324    *    <li class='note'>The returned list is unmodifiable.
1325    * </ul>
1326    *
1327    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1328    * <p class='bjava'>
1329    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>creator</jv> = BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>store</jv>)
1330    *       .debug();
1331    *
1332    *    <jk>try</jk> {
1333      <jv>creator</jv>.run();
1334    *    } <jk>catch</jk> (ExecutableException <jv>e</jv>) {
1335    *       <jc>// Print debug log to understand why creation failed</jc>
1336    *       <jv>creator</jv>.getDebugLog().forEach(System.<jf>err</jf>::println);
1337    *    }
1338    * </p>
1339    *
1340    * @return An unmodifiable list of debug log entries.
1341    */
1342   public List<String> getDebugLog() {
1343      return debug.map(x -> u(copyOf(debug.get()))).orElse(emptyList());
1344   }
1345
1346   /**
1347    * Specifies an existing bean implementation to use instead of creating a new instance.
1348    *
1349    * <p>
1350    * When specified, the bean creation logic is bypassed and this implementation is returned
1351    * directly from {@link #run()}, after performing dependency injection on it.
1352    *
1353    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1354    *    <li class='note'>The bean implementation will still have its fields and methods injected via {@link ClassInfo#inject(Object, BeanStore)}.
1355    *    <li class='note'>This is useful when you want to provide a pre-configured instance but still benefit from dependency injection.
1356    *    <li class='note'>The explicit implementation is preserved even when {@link #reset()} is called, unlike cached results from suppliers.
1357    * </ul>
1358    *
1359    * @param value The bean implementation instance.
1360    * @return This object.
1361    */
1362   public Builder<T> impl(T value) {
1363      this.explicitImplementation = value;
1364      return this;
1365   }
1366
1367   /**
1368    * Registers a post-creation hook to run after the bean is created.
1369    *
1370    * <p>
1371    * Post-creation hooks are executed after the bean is instantiated and after dependency injection
1372    * has been performed via {@link ClassInfo#inject(Object, BeanStore)}.
1373    *
1374    * <p>
1375    * Multiple hooks can be registered and they will be executed in the order they were added.
1376    *
1377    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1378    *    <li class='note'>Hooks are executed every time {@link #run()} is called. Since {@link #run()} uses a cached
1379    *       {@link Memoizer}, hooks are only executed once unless {@link #reset()} is called.
1380    *    <li class='note'>Hooks are executed after dependency injection, so all {@code @Inject} fields and methods
1381    *       will have been populated before the hook runs.
1382    *    <li class='note'>Hooks can be used for custom initialization logic, logging, wrapping beans with proxies,
1383    *       or any other post-creation processing.
1384    *    <li class='note'>If a hook throws an exception, bean creation will fail and the exception will propagate to the caller.
1385    *    <li class='note'>The hook receives the fully initialized bean instance as its parameter.
1386    * </ul>
1387    *
1388    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1389    * <p class='bjava'>
1390    *    <jc>// Register multiple post-creation hooks</jc>
1391    *    MyService <jv>service</jv> = BeanInstantiator
1392    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>myBeanStore</jv>)
1393    *       .postCreateHook(<jv>s</jv> -&gt; <jv>s</jv>.initialize())
1394    *       .postCreateHook(<jv>s</jv> -&gt; <jv>s</jv>.loadConfiguration())
1395    *       .postCreateHook(<jv>s</jv> -&gt; <jv>logger</jv>.info(<js>"Created service: "</js> + <jv>s</jv>))
1396      .run();
1397    * </p>
1398    *
1399    * @param hook The post-creation hook to run after bean creation. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1400    * @return This object.
1401    */
1402   public Builder<T> postCreateHook(Consumer<T> hook) {
1403      assertArgNotNull(ARG_hook, hook);
1404      postCreateHooks.add(hook);
1405      return this;
1406   }
1407
1408   /**
1409    * Suppresses warning-level log output from this builder.
1410    *
1411    * <p>
1412    * Useful when {@link #asOptional()} is preferred and you don't want the underlying log noise that
1413    * accompanies fallback or factory-abstention paths.
1414    *
1415    * <p>
1416    * Errors and exceptions are still thrown normally; only descriptive log entries are suppressed.
1417    *
1418    * @return This object.
1419    */
1420   public Builder<T> silent() {
1421      silent = true;
1422      return this;
1423   }
1424
1425   /**
1426    * Sets a human-readable description used in log entries and error messages.
1427    *
1428    * <p>
1429    * Useful when the bean type alone doesn't provide enough context (e.g. multiple instances of the same type).
1430    *
1431    * @param value The description. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1432    * @return This object.
1433    */
1434   public Builder<T> description(String value) {
1435      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
1436      description = value;
1437      return this;
1438   }
1439
1440   /**
1441    * Registers a transformation to apply to the created bean before it is returned.
1442    *
1443    * <p>
1444    * Multiple wrappers may be registered; they are applied in registration order. Each wrapper receives
1445    * the output of the previous wrapper.
1446    *
1447    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1448    * <p class='bjava'>
1449    *    BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, store)
1450    *       .wrap(LoggingProxy::wrap)
1451    *       .wrap(MetricsProxy::wrap)
1452    *       .run();
1453    * </p>
1454    *
1455    * @param wrapper The wrapper. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1456    * @return This object.
1457    */
1458   public Builder<T> wrap(UnaryOperator<T> wrapper) {
1459      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, wrapper);
1460      wrappers.add(wrapper);
1461      return this;
1462   }
1463
1464   /**
1465    * Registers a post-creation validator.
1466    *
1467    * <p>
1468    * The predicate is invoked on the created bean. If it returns <jk>false</jk>, an
1469    * {@link ExecutableException} is thrown with the supplied message.
1470    *
1471    * @param predicate The predicate. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1472    * @param message The error message used when the predicate returns <jk>false</jk>. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1473    * @return This object.
1474    */
1475   public Builder<T> validate(Predicate<T> predicate, String message) {
1476      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, predicate);
1477      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, message);
1478      validators.add(new Validation<>(predicate, message));
1479      return this;
1480   }
1481
1482   /**
1483    * Registers an alternative {@link BeanInstantiator.Builder} to consult if this builder fails to create the bean.
1484    *
1485    * <p>
1486    * Multiple alternatives may be registered. They are tried in registration order. The first one that
1487    * succeeds returns its bean; if all fail, the last exception is propagated.
1488    *
1489    * @param alternative The alternative builder. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1490    * @return This object.
1491    */
1492   public Builder<T> or(Builder<? extends T> alternative) {
1493      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, alternative);
1494      alternatives.add(alternative);
1495      return this;
1496   }
1497
1498   /**
1499    * Registers a callback that is invoked on the builder instance before {@code build()} is called.
1500    *
1501    * <p>
1502    * Multiple initializers may be registered; they are applied in registration order. The callback
1503    * receives the builder instance after it has been retrieved (from the bean store, static factory,
1504    * or constructor) but before any auto-wiring or {@link #injectBuilder() inject-based} processing.
1505    *
1506    * <p>
1507    * The callback is typed as {@code Consumer<Object>} because the builder type is generally not
1508    * statically known at the call site (an unchecked cast will typically be required inside the
1509    * lambda).
1510    *
1511    * @param initializer The initializer. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1512    * @return This object.
1513    */
1514   public Builder<T> builderInitializer(Consumer<Object> initializer) {
1515      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, initializer);
1516      builderInitializers.add(initializer);
1517      return this;
1518   }
1519
1520   /**
1521    * Enables {@code @Inject} / {@code @Autowired} field and method injection on the builder instance.
1522    *
1523    * <p>
1524    * When set, after the builder is retrieved (from the bean store, static factory, or constructor),
1525    * its fields and methods annotated with {@code @Inject} or {@code @Autowired} are auto-resolved
1526    * from the bean store. This is similar to the post-construction injection performed on the
1527    * resulting bean, but applied to the builder.
1528    *
1529    * <p>
1530    * Default is <jk>false</jk> (opt-in).
1531    *
1532    * @return This object.
1533    */
1534   public Builder<T> injectBuilder() {
1535      injectBuilder = true;
1536      return this;
1537   }
1538
1539   /**
1540    * Enables auto-wiring of bean store entries into matching setter methods on the builder.
1541    *
1542    * <p>
1543    * When set, after the builder is retrieved, every public single-argument setter method is
1544    * inspected. If the bean store has a bean of a type assignable to the setter's parameter type,
1545    * the setter is invoked with that bean.
1546    *
1547    * <p>
1548    * If a parameter is of type {@code Supplier<X>} and the bean store has a bean of type {@code X},
1549    * the bean is wrapped in a supplier and passed to the setter (when {@code unwrapSuppliers}
1550    * defaulting to true).
1551    *
1552    * <p>
1553    * Default is <jk>false</jk> (opt-in).
1554    *
1555    * @return This object.
1556    */
1557   public Builder<T> autoWireBuilder() {
1558      autoWireBuilder = true;
1559      return this;
1560   }
1561
1562   /**
1563    * Disables builder-based bean creation entirely.
1564    *
1565    * <p>
1566    * When set, builder detection is skipped and the bean is instantiated directly via static factory
1567    * methods or constructors. This is useful when a Builder class is co-located with the bean type
1568    * (e.g. {@code Foo.Builder}) but the caller wants to use a builder-taking constructor instead.
1569    *
1570    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1571    * <p class='bjava'>
1572    *    BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(BasicSwaggerProvider.<jk>class</jk>, beanStore)
1573    *       .add(SwaggerProvider.Builder.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>this</jv>)
1574    *       .noBuilder()
1575    *       .run();
1576    * </p>
1577    *
1578    * @return This object.
1579    */
1580   public Builder<T> noBuilder() {
1581      noBuilder = true;
1582      return this;
1583   }
1584
1585   /**
1586    * Sets the lifecycle scope of the bean being created.
1587    *
1588    * <p>
1589    * {@link Scope#SINGLETON} is equivalent to calling {@link #cached()};
1590    * {@link Scope#PROTOTYPE} resets the cached flag.
1591    *
1592    * @param value The scope. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1593    * @return This object.
1594    */
1595   public Builder<T> scope(Scope value) {
1596      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
1597      cached = (value == Scope.SINGLETON);
1598      return this;
1599   }
1600
1601   /**
1602    * Returns a copy of this builder with the same configuration.
1603    *
1604    * <p>
1605    * The returned builder shares no mutable state with this one and may be configured independently.
1606    *
1607    * @return A new builder pre-populated with this builder's configuration.
1608    */
1609   public Builder<T> copy() {
1610      var c = new Builder<T>(beanType.inner(), parentStore, name, enclosingInstance);
1611      c.beanSubType = beanSubType;
1612      c.explicitBuilderType = explicitBuilderType;
1613      c.explicitBuilder = explicitBuilder;
1614      c.explicitImplementation = explicitImplementation;
1615      c.postCreateHooks = new ArrayList<>(postCreateHooks);
1616      c.factoryMethodNames = factoryMethodNames;
1617      c.builderMethodNames = builderMethodNames;
1618      c.buildMethodNames = buildMethodNames;
1619      c.builderClassNames = builderClassNames;
1620      c.fallbackSupplier = fallbackSupplier;
1621      c.cached = cached;
1622      c.factoryAbstainOnNull = factoryAbstainOnNull;
1623      c.preferZeroArgConstructor = preferZeroArgConstructor;
1624      c.silent = silent;
1625      c.description = description;
1626      c.wrappers = new ArrayList<>(wrappers);
1627      c.validators = new ArrayList<>(validators);
1628      c.alternatives = new ArrayList<>(alternatives);
1629      c.builderInitializers = new ArrayList<>(builderInitializers);
1630      c.injectBuilder = injectBuilder;
1631      c.autoWireBuilder = autoWireBuilder;
1632      c.autoWireUnwrapSuppliers = autoWireUnwrapSuppliers;
1633      c.noBuilder = noBuilder;
1634      debug.ifPresent(x -> c.debug.set(new ArrayList<>()));
1635      return c;
1636   }
1637
1638   /**
1639    * Holds a single validator predicate plus its error message.
1640    */
1641   private static record Validation<T>(Predicate<T> predicate, String message) {}
1642
1643   /**
1644    * Resets the builder, builderType, and beanImpl suppliers.
1645    *
1646    * <p>
1647    * This causes them to be re-evaluated on the next call to {@link #run()}.
1648    *
1649    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1650    *    <li class='note'>If an explicit builder instance was set via {@link #builder(Object)},
1651    *       it is preserved and not cleared by this method.
1652    *    <li class='note'>If an explicit implementation was set via {@link #impl(Object)},
1653    *       it is preserved and not cleared by this method.
1654    *    <li class='note'>Only the cached results from suppliers are reset, not the configuration itself.
1655    * </ul>
1656    *
1657    * @return This object.
1658    */
1659   public Builder<T> reset() {
1660      if (explicitBuilder == null) {
1661         builderMemoizer.reset();
1662      }
1663      builderType.reset();
1664      if (explicitImplementation == null) {
1665         beanImpl.reset();
1666      }
1667      return this;
1668   }
1669
1670   /**
1671    * Finds and creates the bean implementation.
1672    *
1673    * <p>
1674    * This method contains all the logic for creating beans via builders, factory methods, constructors, or fallbacks.
1675    *
1676    * @return The created bean.
1677    * @throws ExecutableException if bean could not be created.
1678    */
1679   @SuppressWarnings({
1680      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
1681      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
1682      "java:S6541", // Single-threaded context; synchronization unnecessary
1683   })
1684   private T findBeanImpl() {
1685      var store2 = this.store;
1686      var builder2 = explicitBuilder != null ? explicitBuilder : this.builderMemoizer.get();  // Use explicit builder if set, otherwise get from supplier
1687      T bean = null;
1688      var methodComparator = comparing(MethodInfo::getParameterCount).reversed();
1689      var constructorComparator = comparing(ConstructorInfo::getParameterCount).reversed();
1690
1691      boolean builderAttempted = false;
1692      if (builder2 != null) {
1693         log("Builder detected: %s", cn(builder2));
1694
1695         // Initialization hooks: builder initializers, then optional inject, then optional auto-wire.
1696         for (var init : builderInitializers)
1697            init.accept(builder2);
1698         if (injectBuilder) {
1699            log("Injecting builder via @Inject/@Autowired");
1700            inject(builder2);
1701         }
1702         if (autoWireBuilder) {
1703            log("Auto-wiring builder setters from bean store");
1704            autoWireBuilder(builder2, store2);
1705         }
1706
1707         // Look for Builder.build()/create()/get() - REQUIRED
1708         // Uses buildMethodNames configuration (defaults to "build", "create", "get")
1709         // Check declared methods first to get the most specific override, then fall back to public methods
1710         var buildMethod = info(builder2.getClass()).getPublicMethods().stream()
1711            .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
1712            .filter(x -> buildMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
1713            .filter(x -> opt(x).map(x2 -> x2.getReturnType()).filter(x2 -> x2.is(beanSubType.inner()) || x2.isParentOf(beanSubType)).isPresent()) // Accept methods that return beanSubType or a parent type of beanSubType
1714            .filter(x -> x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isInjectAnnotation) ? x.canResolveAllParameters(store2) : x.getParameterCount() == 0)
1715            .sorted(methodComparator)
1716            .findFirst();
1717
1718         if (buildMethod.isPresent()) {
1719            var method = buildMethod.get();
1720            log("Found build method: %s", method.getNameFull());
1721            log("Method declaring class: %s", method.getDeclaringClass().getName());
1722            log("Builder class: %s", cn(builder2));
1723            var returnType = method.getReturnType();
1724            log("Method return type: %s", returnType.getName());
1725            log("Expected beanSubType: %s", beanSubType.getName());
1726
1727            // Check if method has @Inject annotation
1728            boolean hasInject = method.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isInjectAnnotation);
1729            if (hasInject) {
1730               log("Method has @Inject annotation, resolving parameters from bean store");
1731            } else {
1732               log("Method has no parameters, calling without injection");
1733            }
1734
1735            builderAttempted = true;
1736            // Call the builder method
1737            Object builtBean = method.inject(store2, builder2);
1738
1739            // Validate the produced bean (loose-builder mode). We accept the result if either:
1740            //   1. The build method's declared return type is exactly beanSubType, OR
1741            //   2. The runtime instance produced by the build method is assignment-compatible with beanSubType.
1742            // Case 2 supports the legacy pattern where a parent class's Builder.build() declares the parent
1743            // type but constructs a configured subclass at runtime (e.g. DebugEnablement.Builder.build() returning
1744            // a BasicDebugEnablement because the builder's internal type-binding was preset).
1745            // If neither matches, we fall through to factory-method / constructor resolution on beanSubType
1746            // rather than throwing — that lets standard "subclass adds its own constructor" patterns succeed.
1747            if (returnType.is(beanSubType.inner()) || (builtBean != null && beanSubType.inner().isInstance(builtBean))) {
1748               bean = (T)beanType.cast(builtBean);
1749            } else {
1750               log("Builder method %s returned %s but expected %s; falling through to factory methods/constructors", method.getNameFull(), builtBean == null ? "null" : cn(builtBean), beanSubType.getName());
1751            }
1752         }
1753
1754         if (bean != null) {
1755            log("Bean created successfully via builder");
1756            bean = inject(bean);
1757            runPostCreateHooks(bean);
1758            return bean;
1759         }
1760
1761         // Builder was found but no appropriate build method was found
1762         // Try fallback: look for ANY method on builder that returns the exact bean type
1763         log("Attempting Builder.anything()");
