1764         var anyMethod = info(builder2.getClass()).getPublicMethods().stream()
1765            .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
1766            .filter(x -> !buildMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple())) // Skip standard build methods we already checked
1767            .filter(x -> x.getReturnType().is(beanSubType.inner())) // Must return exact beanSubType, not parent
1768            .filter(x -> x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isInjectAnnotation) ? x.canResolveAllParameters(store2) : x.getParameterCount() == 0)
1769            .sorted(methodComparator)
1770            .findFirst();
1771
1772         if (anyMethod.isPresent()) {
1773            var method = anyMethod.get();
1774            log("Found builder method returning bean type: %s", method.getNameFull());
1775            var returnType = method.getReturnType();
1776
1777            // Call the builder method
1778            Object builtBean = method.inject(store2, builder2);
1779
1780            // Builder method must return the exact beanSubType
1781            if (!returnType.is(beanSubType.inner())) {
1782               log("Builder method %s returns %s, but must return %s", method.getNameFull(), returnType.getName(), beanSubType.getName());
1783               throw exex("Builder method {0} returns {1}, but must return {2}. Builder build methods must always return the exact bean subtype being created.", method.getNameFull(), returnType.getName(), beanSubType.getName());
1784            }
1785            bean = (T)beanType.cast(builtBean);
1786
1787            if (bean != null) {
1788               log("Bean created successfully via builder fallback method");
1789               bean = inject(bean);
1790               runPostCreateHooks(bean);
1791               return bean;
1792            }
1793         }
1794
1795         // Check if builder type has ANY method that returns the right type (even if not named build/create/get)
1796         // If so, log that builder was detected but no appropriate build method was found
1797         // Otherwise, log that builder type is invalid
1798         var builderTypeInfo = builderType.get();
1799         boolean hasAnyMethodWithRightReturnType = builderTypeInfo != null && builderTypeInfo.getPublicMethods().stream()
1800            .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
1801            .map(x -> x.getReturnType())
1802            .anyMatch(x -> x.isAssignableFrom(beanSubType));
1803         boolean hasBuildMethodWithRightReturnType = builderTypeInfo != null && builderTypeInfo.getPublicMethods().stream()
1804            .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
1805            .filter(x -> buildMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
1806            .map(x -> x.getReturnType())
1807            .anyMatch(x -> x.isAssignableFrom(beanSubType));
1808         boolean isExplicitBuilder = explicitBuilderType != null || explicitBuilder != null;
1809         boolean isValidBuilder = builderTypeInfo != null && isValidBuilderType(builderTypeInfo);
1810
1811         if (hasAnyMethodWithRightReturnType && !hasBuildMethodWithRightReturnType) {
1812            // Builder has a method returning the right type but not with the expected name
1813            log("Builder detected but no appropriate build method found. Builder type: %s. Expected method names: %s", cn(builder2), buildMethodNames);
1814            // Fall through to factory methods/constructors
1815         } else if (builderAttempted) {
1816            // We tried the builder's build method but it produced a runtime instance that wasn't the expected
1817            // beanSubType (loose-builder fallthrough above).  Don't throw; fall through to factory-method /
1818            // constructor resolution on beanSubType so subclasses with their own constructor/factory still resolve.
1819            log("Builder %s build method produced wrong runtime type; falling through to factory methods/constructors", cn(builder2));
1820         } else if (hasBuildMethodWithRightReturnType || isExplicitBuilder || isValidBuilder) {
1821            // Builder has a build method with right return type (even if can't be called) or was explicitly set
1822            // Throw exception unless fallback exists
1823            log("Builder detected but no appropriate build method found. Builder type: %s. Expected method names: %s", cn(builder2), buildMethodNames);
1824
1825            if (fallbackSupplier != null) {
1826               log(CONST_usingFallbackSupplier);
1827               T fallbackBean = fallbackSupplier.get();
1828               runPostCreateHooks(fallbackBean);
1829               return fallbackBean;
1830            }
1831
1832            log("Failed to create bean using builder");
1833            throw exex("Could not instantiate class {0} using builder type {1}. Builder must have a build(), create(), or get() method that returns {0}. The method may have @Inject annotation to allow injected parameters.", beanSubType.getName(), builderTypeInfo != null ? builderTypeInfo.getName() : cn(builder2));
1834         } else {
1835            // Builder type is invalid and was auto-detected - fall through to factory methods/constructors
1836            log("Builder detected but builder type is invalid. Builder type: %s. Falling back to factory methods/constructors.", cn(builder2));
1837         }
1838      }
1839
1840      // Look for Bean.factoryMethod().
1841      log("Attempting Bean.factoryMethod()");
1842      // If builder was detected but has no build method, pass it as extra bean for factory methods
1843      Object[] factoryMethodExtraBeans = builder2 != null ? new Object[]{builder2} : new Object[0];
1844      var factoryMethod = beanSubType.getPublicMethods().stream()
1845         .filter(x -> x.isAll(STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
1846         .filter(x -> factoryMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
1847         .filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(beanSubType))
1848         .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(store2, factoryMethodExtraBeans))
1849         .sorted(methodComparator)
1850         .findFirst();
1851      if (factoryMethod.isPresent()) {
1852         log("Found factory method: %s", factoryMethod.get().getNameFull());
1853         bean = (T) beanType.cast(factoryMethod.get().inject(store2, null, factoryMethodExtraBeans));
1854         // When factoryAbstainOnNull is set, treat a null result from the factory method as a
1855         // deliberate "abstain" signal and return null without falling through to constructor
1856         // lookup.  Used by callers like RestOpArg subclasses whose create(ParameterInfo)
1857         // returns null when the parameter isn't annotated with the marker the subclass
1858         // handles (e.g. AttributeArg.create() returns null when @Attr isn't present).
1859         if (bean == null && factoryAbstainOnNull)
1860            return null;
1861      }
1862
1863      // Look for Bean().
1864      // Skip constructor invocation when beanSubType is abstract or an interface — Class.newInstance() would
1865      // throw InstantiationException, masking the more useful "class is abstract"/"class is an interface"
1866      // fallthrough below (which honors the registered fallback supplier when present).
1867      if (bean == null && ! beanSubType.isInterface() && ! beanSubType.isAbstract()) {
1868         log("Attempting Bean() constructor");
1869         // If preferZeroArgConstructor is set and a no-arg constructor exists, short-circuit to it.
1870         // This skips the longest-resolvable-constructor heuristic for callers that just want a fresh
1871         // empty instance — see preferZeroArgConstructor() Javadoc for the parameterized-container rationale.
1872         if (preferZeroArgConstructor) {
1873            var zeroArgCtor = beanSubType.getPublicConstructors().stream()
1874               .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_DEPRECATED))
1875               .filter(x -> x.isDeclaringClass(beanSubType))
1876               .filter(x -> x.getParameterCount() == 0)
1877               .findFirst();
1878            if (zeroArgCtor.isPresent()) {
1879               log("Using zero-arg constructor (preferZeroArgConstructor): %s", zeroArgCtor.get().getNameFull());
1880               bean = (T) beanType.cast(zeroArgCtor.get().inject(store2, enclosingInstance));
1881            }
1882         }
1883         // If builder was detected but has no build method, pass it as extra bean for constructors
1884         Object[] constructorExtraBeans = builder2 != null ? new Object[]{builder2} : new Object[0];
1885         if (bean == null) {
1886            bean = beanSubType.getPublicConstructors().stream()
1887               .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_DEPRECATED))
1888               .filter(x -> x.isDeclaringClass(beanSubType))
1889               .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans))
1890               .sorted(constructorComparator)
1891               .findFirst()
1892               .map(x -> {
1893                  log("Found constructor: %s", x.getNameFull());
1894                  return (T)beanType.cast(x.inject(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans));
1895               })
1896               .orElse(null);
1897         }
1898         // Fallback: try protected constructors (legacy BeanCreator behavior).
1899         // Many framework classes use a protected (Builder) constructor with public static create() factory.
1900         if (bean == null) {
1901            bean = beanSubType.getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
1902               .filter(x -> x.isAll(PROTECTED, NOT_DEPRECATED))
1903               .filter(x -> x.isDeclaringClass(beanSubType))
1904               .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans))
1905               .sorted(constructorComparator)
1906               .findFirst()
1907               .map(x -> {
1908                  log("Found protected constructor: %s", x.getNameFull());
1909                  return (T)beanType.cast(x.inject(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans));
1910               })
1911               .orElse(null);
1912         }
1913         // Fallback: try package-private constructors.
1914         // Useful for @Configuration classes and other framework types that intentionally restrict
1915         // instantiation to their declaring package but still want to participate in the bean store.
1916         // Private constructors are deliberately excluded — they signal "do not instantiate".
1917         if (bean == null) {
1918            bean = beanSubType.getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
1919               .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_PUBLIC, NOT_PROTECTED, NOT_PRIVATE, NOT_DEPRECATED))
1920               .filter(x -> x.isDeclaringClass(beanSubType))
1921               .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans))
1922               .sorted(constructorComparator)
1923               .findFirst()
1924               .map(x -> {
1925                  log("Found package-private constructor: %s", x.getNameFull());
1926                  return (T)beanType.cast(x.inject(store2, enclosingInstance, constructorExtraBeans));
1927               })
1928               .orElse(null);
1929         }
1930      }
1931
1932      if (bean != null) {
1933         log("Bean created successfully");
1934         bean = inject(bean);
1935         runPostCreateHooks(bean);
1936         return bean;
1937      }
1938
1939      if (beanSubType.isInterface()) {
1940         log("Bean type is an interface");
1941         if (fallbackSupplier != null) {
1942            log(CONST_usingFallbackSupplier);
1943            T fallbackBean = fallbackSupplier.get();
1944            runPostCreateHooks(fallbackBean);
1945            return fallbackBean;
1946         }
1947         throw exex("Could not instantiate class {0}: {1}.", beanSubType.getName(), "Class is an interface");
1948      }
1949
1950      if (beanSubType.isAbstract()) {
1951         log("Bean type is abstract");
1952         if (fallbackSupplier != null) {
1953            log(CONST_usingFallbackSupplier);
1954            T fallbackBean = fallbackSupplier.get();
1955            runPostCreateHooks(fallbackBean);
1956            return fallbackBean;
1957         }
1958         throw exex("Could not instantiate class {0}: {1}.", beanSubType.getName(), "Class is abstract");
1959      }
1960
1961      if (fallbackSupplier != null) {
1962         log(CONST_usingFallbackSupplier);
1963         T fallbackBean = fallbackSupplier.get();
1964         runPostCreateHooks(fallbackBean);
1965         return fallbackBean;
1966      }
1967
1968      log("Failed to create bean: no methods/constructors found with matching parameters");
1969      throw exex("Could not instantiate class {0}:  No methods/constructors found with matching parameters", beanSubType.getName());
1970   }
1971
1972   /**
1973    * Finds all bean subtypes, including beanSubType, beanType, and all classes in between.
1974    *
1975    * <p>
1976    * This method traverses the hierarchy from {@code beanSubType} up to {@code beanType}, collecting all classes
1977    * in the inheritance chain. This allows parent bean types to be resolved as injectable types.
1978    *
1979    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1980    * <p class='bjava'>
1981    *    <jc>// Class hierarchy</jc>
1982    *    <jk>class</jk> A { }
1983    *    <jk>class</jk> B <jk>extends</jk> A { }
1984    *    <jk>class</jk> C <jk>extends</jk> B { }
1985    *
1986    *    <jc>// When creating C with beanType=A, beanSubType=C</jc>
1987    *    <jc>// This method returns: [C, B, A]</jc>
1988    * </p>
1989    *
1990    * @return A list of bean subtypes from beanSubType to beanType, never <jk>null</jk>.
1991    */
1992   private List<ClassInfo> findBeanSubTypes() {
1993      var result = new ArrayList<ClassInfo>();
1994
1995      // If beanSubType is the same as beanType, just return a single-element list
1996      if (beanSubType.is(beanType.inner())) {
1997         result.add(beanSubType);
1998         return result;
1999      }
2000
2001      // Traverse from beanSubType up to beanType
2002      var parents = beanSubType.getParents();
2003      for (var parent : parents) {
2004         result.add(parent);
2005         // Stop when we reach beanType
2006         if (parent.is(beanType.inner())) {
2007            break;
2008         }
2009      }
2010
2011      // This should never happen if beanSubType validation is correct, but check anyway
2012      if (result.isEmpty() || !result.get(result.size() - 1).is(beanType.inner())) {
2013         throw illegalArg("beanType {0} was not found in the parent hierarchy of beanSubType {1}. This indicates a validation error.", beanType.getName(), beanSubType.getName());
2014      }
2015
2016      return result;
2017   }
2018
2019   private Object findBuilder() {
2020
2021      // noBuilder() short-circuits all builder detection.
2022      if (noBuilder)
2023         return null;
2024
2025      var bs = store;
2026      var builderType2 = this.builderType.get();
2027
2028      // If no builder type was determined, return null (builder not needed)
2029      if (builderType2 == null)
2030         return null;
2031
2032      Optional<Object> r;
2033
2034      // Step 0: Look for a pre-registered builder of the builder type in the bean store.
2035      // This handles scenarios where the caller has already configured a builder externally
2036      // (e.g. legacy framework code that registers a configured builder bean).
2037      r = bs.getBean(builderType2.inner()).map(Object.class::cast);
2038      if (r.isPresent()) {
2039         log("Using pre-registered builder from bean store: %s", builderType2.getName());
2040         return r.get();
2041      }
2042
2043      // Step 1: Look for a static create/builder method that returns the builder type
2044      r = beanSubType.getPublicMethods().stream()
2045         .filter(x -> x.isAll(STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2046         .filter(x -> builderMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2047         .filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(builderType2))
2048         .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(bs, enclosingInstance))
2049         .sorted(comparing(MethodInfo::getParameterCount).reversed())
2050         .findFirst()
2051         .map(x -> inject(x.inject(bs, enclosingInstance)));
2052      if (r.isPresent())
2053         return r.get();
2054
2055      // Step 2: Look for a public constructor on the builder type
2056      r = builderType2.getPublicConstructors().stream()
2057         .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_DEPRECATED))
2058         .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(bs, enclosingInstance))
2059         .sorted(comparing(ConstructorInfo::getParameterCount).reversed())
2060         .findFirst()
2061         .map(x -> inject(x.inject(bs, enclosingInstance)));
2062
2063      if (r.isPresent())
2064         return r.get();
2065
2066      // Step 3: Look for a protected constructor on the builder type
2067      r = builderType2.getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
2068         .filter(x -> x.isAll(ElementFlag.PROTECTED, NOT_DEPRECATED))
2069         .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(bs, enclosingInstance))
2070         .sorted(comparing(ConstructorInfo::getParameterCount).reversed())
2071         .findFirst()
2072         .map(x -> inject(x.inject(bs, enclosingInstance)));
2073      if (r.isPresent())
2074         return r.get();
2075
2076      // Step 4: Look for a package-private constructor on the builder type.
2077      // Mirrors the package-private fallback in findBeanImpl() for symmetry: any builder co-located in
2078      // the bean's declaring package can be discovered without forcing the constructor to be public/protected.
2079      r = builderType2.getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
2080         .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_PUBLIC, NOT_PROTECTED, NOT_PRIVATE, NOT_DEPRECATED))
2081         .filter(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(bs, enclosingInstance))
2082         .sorted(comparing(ConstructorInfo::getParameterCount).reversed())
2083         .findFirst()
2084         .map(x -> inject(x.inject(bs, enclosingInstance)));
2085
2086      return r.orElse(null);
2087   }
2088
2089   /**
2090    * Finds the builder type based on priority:
2091    * 1. Explicitly set builderType
2092    * 2. @Builder annotation on beanSubType (includes inherited annotations)
2093    * 3. Autodetect from static methods or inner classes
2094    */
2095   @SuppressWarnings({
2096      "unchecked",  // Type erasure requires cast to ClassInfo for builder type
2097      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for multi-strategy builder-type resolution
2098   })
2099   private ClassInfo findBuilderType() {
2100      log("Finding builder type...");
2101
2102      // noBuilder() short-circuits all builder detection.
2103      if (noBuilder) {
2104         log("noBuilder() set; skipping builder type detection");
2105         return null;
2106      }
2107
2108      // Priority 1: Explicitly set builderType
2109      if (explicitBuilderType != null) {
2110         log("Using explicitly set builder type: %s", explicitBuilderType.getName());
2111         return explicitBuilderType;
2112      }
2113
2114      // Priority 2: @Builder annotation on beanSubType
2115      // Check declared annotations first to ensure child's annotation overrides parent's
2116      var r = beanSubType.getDeclaredAnnotations().stream()
2117         .filter(a -> a.isType(org.apache.juneau.commons.Builder.class))
2118         .map(a -> (AnnotationInfo<org.apache.juneau.commons.Builder>)a)
2119         .map(x -> ClassInfo.class.cast(info(x.inner().value())))
2120         .findFirst();
2121      if (r.isPresent()) {
2122         log("Found builder via @Builder annotation on beanSubType: %s", r.get().getName());
2123         return r.get();
2124      }
2125      // Fall back to inherited annotations if no declared annotation found
2126      r = beanSubType.getAnnotations(org.apache.juneau.commons.Builder.class)
2127         .map(x -> ClassInfo.class.cast(info(x.inner().value())))
2128         .findFirst();
2129      if (r.isPresent()) {
2130         log("Found builder via @Builder annotation (inherited): %s", r.get().getName());
2131         return r.get();
2132      }
2133
2134      // Priority 3: Autodetect
2135      // 3a: Look for static create/builder method that returns a potential builder type.
2136      var staticBuilderTypes = beanSubType.getPublicMethods().stream()
2137         .filter(x -> x.isAll(STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2138         .filter(x -> builderMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2139         .filter(x -> ! x.hasReturnType(beanSubType)) // Must not return the bean type itself
2140         .filter(x -> isValidBuilderType(x.getReturnType()))
2141         .map(MethodInfo::getReturnType)
2142         .toList();
2143      if (! staticBuilderTypes.isEmpty()) {
2144         // Prefer a factory whose builder builds the EXACT requested type (or a
2145         // subtype) over one that only promises a supertype — e.g. an inherited generic builder(Class)
2146         // whose build() erases to a base class. A supertype-only factory must not displace a stricter path.
2147         var strict = staticBuilderTypes.stream().filter(this::isStrictBuilderType).findFirst();
2148         if (strict.isPresent()) {
2149            log("Found builder via static method (strict): %s", strict.get().getName());
2150            return strict.get();
2151         }
2152         var weak = gateWeakBuilder(staticBuilderTypes.get(0));
2153         if (weak != null) {
2154            log("Found builder via static method: %s", weak.getName());
2155            return weak;
2156         }
2157         // Weak/supertype-only static factory rejected in favor of a direct constructor; fall through to
2158         // 3b/3c (a stricter inner Builder may still exist) and ultimately the constructor path.
2159      }
2160
2161      // 3b: Look for inner Builder class
2162      log("Looking for inner builder class (names: %s)...", builderClassNames);
2163      r = beanSubType.getDeclaredMemberClasses().stream()
2164         .filter(x -> builderClassNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2165         .findFirst();
2166      if (r.isPresent()) {
2167         var builderClass = r.get();
2168         if (isValidBuilderType(builderClass)) {
2169            var gated = isStrictBuilderType(builderClass) ? builderClass : gateWeakBuilder(builderClass);
2170            if (gated != null) {
2171               log("Found builder via inner class: %s", gated.getName());
2172               return gated;
2173            }
2174            // Weak inner builder rejected in favor of a direct constructor; fall through.
2175         } else {
2176            // Still return it so we can provide a better error message when trying to use it
2177            log("Found builder class via inner class but it is not valid: %s", builderClass.getName());
2178            return builderClass;
2179         }
2180      }
2181
2182      // 3c: Look for builder in parent classes and implemented interfaces (skip beanSubType itself,
2183      // which was already searched in 3b). getAllParents returns parents (child-to-parent) followed by
2184      // interfaces, so we walk the whole chain in a uniform order.
2185      var allParents = beanSubType.getAllParents();
2186      for (int i = 1; i < allParents.size(); i++) {
2187         var parentClass = allParents.get(i);
2188         r = parentClass.getDeclaredMemberClasses().stream()
2189            .filter(x -> builderClassNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2190            .findFirst();
2191         if (r.isPresent()) {
2192            var builderClass = r.get();
2193            if (isValidBuilderType(builderClass)) {
2194               var gated = isStrictBuilderType(builderClass) ? builderClass : gateWeakBuilder(builderClass);
2195               if (gated != null) {
2196                  log("Found builder via parent inner class: %s", gated.getName());
2197                  return gated;
2198               }
2199               // Weak parent inner builder rejected in favor of a direct constructor; keep scanning.
2200               continue;
2201            }
2202            // Still return it so we can provide a better error message when trying to use it
2203            log("Found builder class via parent inner class but it is not valid: %s", builderClass.getName());
2204            return builderClass;
2205         }
2206      }
2207
2208      // No builder type found
2209      log("No builder type found");
2210      return null;
2211   }
2212
2213   /**
2214    * Tests whether a builder candidate builds the <b>exact</b> requested bean type (or a subtype of it),
2215    * as opposed to only promising a supertype.
2216    *
2217    * <p>
2218    * Used by {@link #findBuilderType()} to prefer a precise builder over an inherited
2219    * generic base builder whose {@code build()} erases to a parent class (e.g. a self-typed REST
2220    * {@code DefaultBuilder} whose {@code build()} returns {@code RestMixin}).
2221    *
2222    * @param builderCandidate The builder type to test.
2223    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the candidate has a 0-arg (or {@code @Inject}) build/create/get method whose
2224    *    return type is {@code beanSubType} or a subtype of it.
2225    */
2226   private boolean isStrictBuilderType(ClassInfo builderCandidate) {
2227      return builderCandidate.getPublicMethods().stream()
2228         .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2229         .filter(x -> buildMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2230         .filter(x -> { var rt = x.getReturnType(); return rt.is(beanSubType.inner()) || beanSubType.isParentOf(rt); })
2231         .anyMatch(x -> x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isInjectAnnotation) || x.getParameterCount() == 0);
2232   }
2233
2234   /**
2235    * A builder candidate that only builds a <i>supertype</i> of the requested bean
2236    * type must not be used when the requested type is concretely instantiable via a direct constructor.
2237    *
2238    * <p>
2239    * Returns the candidate unchanged when it is safe to use, or {@code null} to signal "prefer the
2240    * constructor path". This is only invoked for non-strict (supertype-only) candidates.
2241    *
2242    * @param builderCandidate The (weak) builder candidate.
2243    * @return The candidate, or {@code null} to prefer the direct-constructor path.
2244    */
2245   private ClassInfo gateWeakBuilder(ClassInfo builderCandidate) {
2246      if (hasUsableDirectConstructor()) {
2247         log("Builder candidate %s only builds a supertype of %s and a usable direct constructor exists; preferring the constructor.", builderCandidate.getName(), beanSubType.getName());
2248         return null;
2249      }
2250      return builderCandidate;
2251   }
2252
2253   /**
2254    * Tests whether the requested bean type is concretely instantiable via a non-private constructor whose
2255    * parameters can all be resolved from the bean store (a no-arg constructor always qualifies).
2256    *
2257    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a usable direct constructor exists on {@code beanSubType}.
2258    */
2259   private boolean hasUsableDirectConstructor() {
2260      if (beanSubType.isAbstract() || beanSubType.isInterface())
2261         return false;
2262      return beanSubType.getDeclaredConstructors().stream()
2263         .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_PRIVATE))
2264         .filter(x -> x.isDeclaringClass(beanSubType))
2265         .anyMatch(x -> x.canResolveAllParameters(store, enclosingInstance));
2266   }
2267
2268   /**
2269    * Finds all builder types, including the primary builder type and any builder types found in the builder's parent hierarchy.
2270    *
2271    * <p>
2272    * This method traverses the parent hierarchy of the primary builder type, looking for builder classes whose build methods
2273    * return types that are the same as or parents of {@code beanType}. This allows parent builders to be resolved as injectable types.
2274    *
2275    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2276    * <p class='bjava'>
2277    *    <jc>// Class A with builder</jc>
2278    *    <jk>class</jk> A {
2279    *       <jk>static class</jk> Builder {
2280    *          A create() { <jk>return new</jk> A(); }
2281    *       }
2282    *    }
2283    *
2284    *    <jc>// Class B extends A with builder</jc>
2285    *    <jk>class</jk> B <jk>extends</jk> A {
2286    *       <jk>static class</jk> Builder <jk>extends</jk> A.Builder {
2287    *          B create() { <jk>return new</jk> B(); }
2288    *       }
2289    *    }
2290    *
2291    *    <jc>// When creating B, this method returns: [B.Builder, A.Builder]</jc>
2292    *    <jc>// Traverses B.Builder → A.Builder hierarchy</jc>
2293    *    <jc>// B.Builder.create() returns B (matches beanType)</jc>
2294    *    <jc>// A.Builder.create() returns A (A is parent of B)</jc>
2295    * </p>
2296    *
2297    * @return A list of builder types, never <jk>null</jk>.
2298    */
2299   private List<ClassInfo> findBuilderTypes() {
2300      var result = new ArrayList<ClassInfo>();
2301
2302      // Start with the primary builder type
2303      var primaryBuilderType = findBuilderType();
2304      if (primaryBuilderType == null) {
2305         return result;
2306      }
2307
2308      // Add the primary builder type first
2309      result.add(primaryBuilderType);
2310
2311      // Traverse the parent hierarchy of the builder type itself (skip the first element which is the primary builder type itself)
2312      var builderParents = primaryBuilderType.getParents();
2313      for (int i = 0; i < builderParents.size(); i++) {
2314         var builderParent = builderParents.get(i);
2315         builderParent.getPublicMethods().stream()
2316            .filter(m -> m.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2317            .filter(m -> buildMethodNames.contains(m.getNameSimple()))
2318            .filter(m -> m.getReturnType().isParentOf(beanType))
2319            .findFirst()
2320            .ifPresent(x -> result.add(builderParent));
2321      }
2322
2323      return result;
2324   }
2325
2326   /**
2327    * Helper method to inject beans into an object instance.
2328    *
2329    * @param <T> The bean type.
2330    * @param bean The object to inject beans into.
2331    * @return The same bean instance (for method chaining).
2332    */
2333   private <T2> T2 inject(T2 bean) {
2334      return ClassInfo.of(bean).inject(bean, store);
2335   }
2336
2337   /**
2338    * Auto-wires bean store entries into matching public single-argument setter methods of the builder.
2339    *
2340    * <p>
2341    * Setters are public, non-static, non-deprecated methods taking exactly one argument. The bean store
2342    * is queried for a bean assignable to the parameter's type. If the parameter is {@code Supplier<X>}
2343    * and {@link #autoWireUnwrapSuppliers} is true, the bean of type {@code X} is wrapped before being
2344    * passed in.
2345    *
2346    * <p>
2347    * Setters whose argument cannot be resolved are silently skipped.
2348    */
2349   @SuppressWarnings({
2350      "java:S3776", // Best-effort auto-wiring branches on reflection and type-shape checks.
2351      "java:S3011" // Reflection access override is required for invoking discovered setters uniformly.
2352   })
2353   private void autoWireBuilder(Object builder2, BeanStore bs) {
2354      var ci = info(builder2.getClass());
2355      ci.getPublicMethods().stream()
2356         .filter(m -> m.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2357         .filter(m -> m.getParameterCount() == 1)
2358         .forEach(m -> {
2359            try {
2360               var p0 = m.getParameterTypes().get(0);
2361               Class<?> rawType = p0.inner();
2362               Object value = null;
2363               if (autoWireUnwrapSuppliers && Supplier.class.isAssignableFrom(rawType)) {
2364                  // Best-effort: try to resolve the wrapped type from the parameter's parameterized signature.
2365                  var generic = m.inner().getGenericParameterTypes()[0];
2366                  if (generic instanceof java.lang.reflect.ParameterizedType pt) {
2367                     var args = pt.getActualTypeArguments();
2368                     if (args.length == 1 && args[0] instanceof Class<?> wrapped) {
2369                        var wrappedBean = bs.getBean(wrapped);
2370                        if (wrappedBean.isPresent()) {
2371                           Object wb = wrappedBean.get();
2372                           value = (Supplier<Object>) () -> wb;
2373                        }
2374                     }
2375                  }
2376               } else {
2377                  var bean = bs.getBean(rawType);
2378                  if (bean.isPresent())
2379                     value = bean.get();
2380               }
2381               if (value != null) {
2382                  m.inner().setAccessible(true);
2383                  m.inner().invoke(builder2, value);
2384                  log("Auto-wired setter %s with bean of type %s", m.getNameSimple(), rawType.getName());
2385               }
2386            } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
2387               // Swallow auto-wire errors silently; auto-wire is a best-effort.
2388            }
2389         });
2390   }
2391
2392   /**
2393    * Checks if a type can be used as a builder.
2394    * A valid builder type must have:
2395    * - A build/create/get method that returns the bean type or a parent of the bean type
2396    *   - The method may have @Inject annotation to allow injected parameters
2397    *   - If no @Inject annotation, the method must have no parameters
2398    */
2399   private boolean isValidBuilderType(ClassInfo builderCandidate) {
2400      log("Validating builder candidate: %s", builderCandidate.getName());
2401
2402      // Check if it has a build/create/get method that returns beanSubType, beanType, or a parent of beanSubType
2403      var hasBuildMethod = builderCandidate.getPublicMethods().stream()
2404         .filter(x -> x.isAll(NOT_STATIC, NOT_DEPRECATED, NOT_SYNTHETIC, NOT_BRIDGE))
2405         .filter(x -> buildMethodNames.contains(x.getNameSimple()))
2406         .filter(x -> {
2407            var returnType = x.getReturnType();
2408            return returnType.is(beanSubType.inner()) || returnType.is(beanType.inner()) || returnType.isParentOf(beanSubType);
2409         })
2410         .anyMatch(x -> x.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isInjectAnnotation) || x.getParameterCount() == 0);
2411
2412      if (hasBuildMethod) {
2413         log("Builder is valid: has build/create/get method returning bean type");
2414         return true;
2415      }
2416
2417      log("Builder is NOT valid: no build/create/get method returning bean type");
2418      return false;
2419   }
2420
2421   private void log(String message, Object... args) {
2422      if (silent && ! debug.isPresent())
2423         return;
2424      var prefix = description != null ? (description + " (" + beanType.getName() + ")") : beanType.getName();
2425      logger.fine(prefix + ": " + message, args);
2426      debug.ifPresent(x -> x.add(args.length == 0 ? message : String.format(message, args)));
2427   }
2428
2429   T runPostCreateHooks(T bean) {
2430      postCreateHooks.forEach(x -> x.accept(bean));
2431      return bean;
2432   }
2433   } // class Builder
2434}
